title: Cookiemonster
author: bernhard
description: Grainsynth Sampler based on the Example in the Documentation. I'd call it crumblesynthesis. "ME WANT COOKIE!!!"

title: Cookiemonster Auto-Variant
author: Johannes (Variante von bernhards Cookiemonster, 1-5d3)
description: Granular-Sampler-Variante ohne MouseXY-Control. Statt MouseX/Y fuer Playback-Rate und Buffer-Position laufen jetzt zwei LFNoise1 langsam von alleine durch sinnvolle Bereiche. Damit ist's ein generativer Granular-Drone den man einfach "anwir

title: droneFM01
author: Oscar Recarte
description: Polyrhythmic and dynamic FM drone. More detailed info (in Spanish) at https://recarteblog.wordpress.com/2021/02/23/example-post-2/

title: Meandering Sines
author: emergent
description: 8 sine waves, each with a slightly meandering frequency, random phase, slowly varying amplitude, slowly wandering through the stereo field; pitches are chosen from a given chord that is changing at intervals between 18 and 20 seconds.

title: Paulstretch for SuperCollider
author: jpdrecourt
description: This is a port of the basic Paulstretch algorithm to SuperCollider (no onset detection). Mono version, for stereo, use 2 instances hard panned. The sound buffer needs to be mono too, so use Buffer.readChannel to extract separate channels. T

title: 9/8 drum'n strings
author: bernhard
description: An experiment with euclidian patterns, sampled drums and synthesized strings. Inspired by middle eastern 9/8 rhythms. Replace the samples in the dictionary with whatever percussion sounds you have.

title: Hotroded "08091500Acid309 by_otophilia
author: bernhard
description: Hotroded version of "08091500Acid309 by_otophilia". With additional basssynth, euclidian patterns (needs the Bjorklund Object). With some randomisation for bass- and leadsynth.

title: friture plus
author: bernhard
description: Random deep fryer. Can turn in to rain, waves and other stuff.

title: Hijacked Tweets in Ndef.
author: bernhard
description: Variations of hijacked tweets. In Ndef-mode. Starting point was nathaniel virgo's tweet: {a=LFTri.ar(1);20.do{a=BAllPass.ar(a,80,1);a=((a+0.02)*LFNoise0.kr(1/2)*8).tanh;a=LeakDC.ar(a,0.995)};a*0.1!2}.play

title: Noise bursts and clicks going through ringing filters
author: blaz.pavlica
description: A bunch noise bursts and clicks going through ringing filters, sequenced with patterns. marimba and wood SynthDefs are taken from this blog https://thormagnusson.gitbooks.io/scoring/content/PartII/chapter11.html Needs ChordSymbol quark to w

title: Polyswing_asym
author: bernhard
description: Rhythm-Experiment for sambalike patterns. The goal are cyclic patterns that accelerate in asymetrical way inside one cycle.

title: [SIMPLE] Random Pad Generator
author: Checco Ruseo
description: A random pad generation thing. Every time you click EXECUTE, a pad with a certain number of melodic lines is generated using the parameters in the GUI. Use it -> study the code -> modify it -> ? -> profit! P.S. sorry for my mediocre english

title: blipecho context
author: vividsnow
description: sample mash-up from "Funky Klangs" and "Re: Dub Echo"

title: Re: Dub Echo using LocalIn + LocalOut (and Ping Pong)
author: rukano
description: Fork of Bjorn's Dub echo, rewritten w/o the Feedback Quark (using LocalIn + LocalOut) and adding a ping pong (can be removed by deleting the .reverse method in the LocalOut line)

title: Re: Dub Echo with Ping Pong
author: rukano
description: Taking the Dub echo by Bjorn and putting some ping poing to it :)

title: Echo + Feedback Tweet
author: Schemawound
code: {t=Mix.fill(3,{|i|SinOsc.ar(1000+(i*70))})/8;t=Streson.ar(t,SinOsc.kr(0.1).range(0.1,1),1);t=CombN.ar(t,30,0.7,30)+t;t=t!2 * 0.05}.play

title: Feedback Ambient
author: rumush
description: Simple feedback Ndef that can produce variety of sounds by just changing two variables, freq and rt. Feel free to modify the code and let me know what you come up with.

title: fun little instrument
author: whillas
description: Simple Pulse with some delayed feedback and a 2D Slider as an interface. Sliding around gives different effects to just clicking in different spots.

title: Drunken Dwarf
author: Melas Onos
description: Just some weird thing made with some physical modelling gone wrong

title: Simple FM template with additive mixing of modulators
author: LFSaw
description: Template to create a 2x3 FM Synth. There are two independent FM synths, of type `car(mod1(mod2))`. all carriers and modulators are collected together and are mixed to the output according to `adLevels`. This is intended to be a starting poi

title: Simple FM template
author: LFSaw
description: Template to create a 2x3 FM Synth. There are two independent FM synths, of type `car(mod1(mod2))`. This is intended to be a starting point for further exploration.

title: gongFM_SC
author: Oscar Recarte
description: Various gong-like sounds using FM synthesis. More detailed info (in Spanish) at https://recarteblog.wordpress.com/2021/05/05/gongfm_sc/

title: 4 operator phase modulation synth
author: david_morgan
description: Note: you can configure more than 4 operators by changing the value of the numops variable.

title: Stylophone tone simulation
author: na
description: Basic tone simulator of a stylophone. Sugestions//improvements are welcome :)

title: Boreas
author: Alexander Zhagun-Linnik
description: The second piece in this generative series. Note lengths are now variable within a line, but as the result the total duration of a pattern became variable too so lines are not always playing in sync. This, however, adds to overall unpredict

title: deta-nocturn
author: LFSaw
description: A piece experimenting with the settling of 8-bit programs running in BetaBlocker (Dave Griffith & Till Bovermann, 2011).

title: Re: Generating Graphics and Music From The Dragon Curve
author: blueprint
description: These are variations using lsystems I built for: https://anvaka.github.io/lsystem/ I've only changed the LSystem itself, not the note selections or instruments.

title: Digital Harp (and a just intonation demo)
author: akselai
description: Digital concert harp. Requires DWGPlucked2.

title: fraction2basimal
author: eli.rosenkim
description: function that extracts repeating digits in "basimal" representations of ratios

title: monzos + sloth patch
author: eli.rosenkim
description: fun patch using sloth canons to control places of a monzo

title: Monzo function
author: eli.rosenkim
description: function takes in collection as argument, and returns the ratio from the resultant monzo

title: sloth canons
author: eli.rosenkim
description: routine that takes a seed and generates a self-similar sloth canon with it by adding each term to each term, better explained in this blog post: https://www.science20.com/robert_inventor039s_column/blog/self_similar_sloth_canon_number_seque

title: Simple performance script to launch patterns a la Ableton
author: misc29377
description: This script was my first Supercollider project to create a performance tool something similar like Ableton Live. The script sets up a reverb and delay bus, loads samples (Kickdrum, snare, hihat, tom, bass, fx and pad) and creates sequences

title: MIDI arpeggiator demo
author: wondersluyter
description: Roughly based on the stock ableton arpeggiator behavior. A lot of variation is possible combining custom ~chordSort and ~notePat functions.

title: Command-line SC utility for MIDI clock out
author: jamshark70
description: When sending MIDI clock out at a fast tempo, there may be fewer than 10 ms between clock ticks. If you're sending the clock messages from the same sclang that is sequencing musical events, any unexpectedly long-running sclang activity could

title: exhaustive MIDI debugger
author: soundlake
description: Inspired by https://sccode.org/1-5co Written in 3.11.2. All arguments are labeled. Source ID becomes more human-readable format; MIDIEndPoint.device and MIDIEndPoint.name.

title: Controlling an external synth parameters with NRPN / adding LFOs
author: g_montel
description: I bought a Sequential OB-6 recently and you can control all the parameters with NRPN messages. I wanted to control these NRPN parameters with patterns, adding LFOs for instance.

title: alone in the storm
author: grirgz

title: The City Breathing

description: an installation-setup

title: Re: Dark sea horns

description: Something I put together a while back by forking Dark Sea Horns

author: vividsnow
description: wind whirls

title: randochestra
author: vividsnow
description: exploring of morphing cycled envelopes (i.e. kind of grains) as a sound source; give it some tries - there are possibly interesting ones

title: The Tunnel
author: Schemawound
description: This code contains three sections, the first defines the synthdef. The second starts up the synth and the GUI for playing with the presets live. The third is used to render out the completed album version of the song. The song can be downlo

title: 240 plucks
author: Luka P.
description: using Pluck.ar with PinkNoise as input and Patterns - major scale with sequence: 1,3,7,8 and randomly 10,11,13,14 at some weighted random intervals. at 50bpm it becomes quite soothing. Needs JPverb from sc3_plugins.

title: How to build a mixer with send bus in pattern world
author: grirgz

title: how to generate the synthdef with a pattern
author: grirgz
description: synthdef building should take less time than s.latency for this to work

title: simple timeline for patterns
description: simple timeline for scheduling start and stop of patterns at specific beats (and an experimental extension with absolute time scheduling)

title: XoX-Drumkit nach gosub-Pattern
author: Johannes (Variante mit eigener Drum-Stack)
description: Eigene Anwendung des XoX-Helpers aus 1-5hZ. Drei Layer Drums per XoX- String, alle parallel als Ptpar. Demonstriert wie der gosub-Helper in einem typischen Vier-Vier-Setup verwendet wird. === Bezug zum Original (1-5hZ) === Original definier

title: XoX pattern helper
author: gosub
description: a simple helper function to write \dur patterns in a XoX style i.e. Pseq(~xox.("xxooxoxo") * 8/120, inf)

author: Schemawound

author: Jonatan Liljedahl
description: quantized random triggers creates a rhythm that increases in density over the first 30 seconds.

author: Schemawound

title: metallic rhythmic patterns
author: Jonatan Liljedahl
code:
play{Limiter.ar(GVerb.ar(sin(Ringz.ar(Impulse.ar(2**LFNoise0.ar(1!2).range(1,4).round),[40,200,234,889],0.7).sum*2),5,2,0.7))}

play{Limiter.ar(GVerb.ar(sin(Ringz.ar(Impulse.ar(2**LFNoise0.ar(1!3).range(1,5).round/2),[40,200,234,889,1320,150],0.7).sum),5,2,0.7)/2)}

author: xffff

title: Frequency Modulation GUI Demo 1
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Simple interface to experiment with basic frequency modulation (big knobs!)

title: Frequency Modulation GUI Demo 2
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Another interface to experiment with frequency modulation, now using Modulation Index.

title: Frequency Modulation GUI Demo 3
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Experiment with FM by specifying contours for Carrier Frequency, Modulator Frequency, Modulation Index, and Amplitude Envelope. You can also choose duration of "notes".

title: Frequency Modulation GUI Demo 4
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Experiment with FM by specifying contours for Carrier Frequency, Modulation Index, and Amplitude Envelope. Also adjust duration of events. In this version you don't specify Modulator Frequency directly, but rather you specify the Frequency

title: Granular Sampling GUI Demo 1
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Graphical interface to experiment with granular sampling (one sound file at a time).

title: Granular Sampling GUI Demo 2
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Graphical interface to experiment with granular sampling (multiple sound files at a time).

title: Ring Modulation GUI Demo
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Simple interface to experiment with with Ring Modulation (a type of Amplitude Modulation).

title: Subtractive Synthesis GUI Demo 1
author: Bruno Ruviaro
description: Graphical interface to play with subtractive synthesis. Use the 2D slider to choose filter frequency (x-axis) and filter rq (y-axis). Use the menu to choose noise source: Pink Noise, White Noise, Gray Noise, Brown Noise, or Clip Noise.
